text:
This article is about the video game character. You may also be looking for Lyra, a character exclusive to the 2003 anime.

NOTE: This character's first name has never been given an official English translation. The one used in this article may not be correct.
 is a character in the video game Fullmetal Alchemist: Sonata of Memories. She is the younger sister of Viola Amore and ex-lover of Lute Zaxophone. She was turned into a chimera before the events of the story. Note that in Japanese her name is pronounced as lee-rah, meaning her name is likely meant to use the French pronunciation of leer rather than the English pronunciation which is a homophone of the word liar.

Name & Etymology

Her surname, アモーレ, is confirmed to be spelt Amore in English as her sister, Viola Amore, has her full name written in English on her status screen. Her sister's name comes from the instrument viola d'amore. There is no such thing as a "lyre d'amore", but there are various other instruments with d'amore in their name, such as oboe d'amore, clarinet d'amore, etc., giving the surname musical origins in general.

Her first name, リラ, has never officially been translated into English, with there being multiple valid possible translations.
In Japanese, リラ can mean many things. One meaning is lyre, a stringed instrument which is categorised as part of the lute family of instruments. This most likely the intended meaning, as her ex-lover is named Lute, creating a connection with their names. Furthermore, all other newly introduced characters in Sonata of Memories (Viola Amore, Lute Zaxophone, and Claves Fagott) are named after musical instruments.
The instrument lyre can be translated into Japanese as リラ (Rira), リュラ (Ryura), ライアー, (Raiā), or ライア (Raia). The first two are loanwords from French (and therefore use the French pronunciation), while the latter two are loanwords taken from English (and therefore use the English pronunciation).
If translated literally, Lila, Rira, Rila, or Lira would all be valid translations. The English fan translations opts for "Lila Amore" as her name.
Note that the English name Lila, which comes from the flower lilac, is normally pronounced "LAI-luh", but the Japanese characters for her name denotes it would be pronounced with a long e sound, which would make her name pronounced "lee-la". This may make Leila or Leela better choices for translation.
Similarly, the other translations would be pronounced as "ree-rah", "ree-lah", or "lee-rah".

Gallery

 
Lyre Boss.png||Viola confronting her sister Lyre as a chimera. "I'll help you... I'll free you from this state..."
 

Trivia

When fought as a boss, the game forces the player to have a party of Edward, Winry, and Viola, even if the player had used Alphonse, Hawkeye, Mustang, or Alex in the preceding battle.
Her chimeric form seems to be based on an alraune, which is the German word for a female mandrake, a mythological plant-like creature. While mandrakes can be depicted in a variety of ways, the specific term "alraune" in media (especially roleplaying games) usually refers to a creature which has an upper body that resembles a human woman and lower body which resembles a plant, much like Lyre's chimeric form.
If her Japanese name is translated as Lira or Lyra (pronounced lee-rah), her name would be pronounced identically to the way that Lyra's name is pronounced in the English dub of the 2003 anime. This is definitely not intentional, though, as in Japanese the characters' names are pronounced differently. The anime character's is pronounced lai-rah, while the game character's name is pronounced lee-rah.
